notes: Disables MiniMax deferred reductions across nonlinear graph boundaries.
Mirrors materialized client-side reductions back to source shards so downstream per-device branches can consume full activations.
Fixes get_input_tensor_sm_graph() to return the materialized reduce tensor for non-deferred reductions.

runs.label: rpc-graph-nodefer-mirror-pre-inputfix
runs.interpretation: The first attempt showed the split count and then failed because downstream graph nodes still consumed per-device partials from non-deferred reduce outputs.

comparison.branch_fused_graph_note: Faster than the corrected graph smoke but not promoted because deferred reductions cross nonlinear boundaries.

conclusion: Quality-correct MiniMax graph/tensor parallelism needs real reduce/broadcast before nonlinear layer boundaries. The current client-side RPC reduce/mirror path proves correctness-oriented dataflow but is far too slow for steady-state decode.
